jokes.text: No, I didn't send her a get-well card 'cause you don't get a get-well card when you have plastic surgery.
jokes.setup: Dan talking to his mother about sending someone a get-well card
jokes.punchline: The logical but callous distinction about plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment showing Dan's self-absorbed callousness disguised as logic

jokes.text: I'm so tired, I could sleep a horse. Or whatever that word thing is.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to use a common idiom
jokes.punchline: Mangling 'sleep like a horse' and then acknowledging her confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - confident incompetence with meta-awareness

jokes.text: I got all jacked up on licorice last night and I was belly dancing till dawn.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining why he needs sleep medication
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of Ben hyped up on licorice and belly dancing
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd visual combined with Ben's deadpan delivery - highly quotable and memorable

jokes.text: Imagine being detained for two weeks just 'cause you're a journalist and a shithead.
jokes.setup: Mike commenting on Leon's detention
jokes.punchline: Adding 'and a shithead' as an equal factor to being a journalist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike callousness disguised as sympathy - great economy of language

jokes.text: It's a good job we had nothing to do with his being detained. - Yeah, wouldn't that be terrible? - Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Mike's obviously false statement about their innocence
jokes.punchline: Gary's nervous agreement making their guilt more obvious
jokes.explanation: Classic Veep irony - characters revealing guilt through attempted innocence

jokes.text: Wait, did we have something to do with...?
jokes.setup: Gary slowly realizing the implications
jokes.punchline: His dawning horror at what they might have done
jokes.explanation: Gary's naive realization moment - good character work but not exceptional

jokes.text: And you know what amazing men get? - What? - Two-day weekend. - This weekend? - Yeah. - I'm going to the cottage with Wendy!
jokes.setup: Amy building up to reward for 'amazing men'
jokes.punchline: The mundane reward of a normal weekend off
jokes.explanation: Solid setup-punchline with Amy's manipulation and Jonah's excitement

jokes.text: It's the one and only time anyone's ever gonna say that.
jokes.setup: Mike telling Selina she needs a photo with Leon West
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging this is the only time Leon will be worth photographing with
jokes.explanation: Mike's brutal honesty delivered with perfect timing - great economy

jokes.text: Let's cure diabetes or learn Italian.
jokes.setup: Selina wanting to use their extra hour productively
jokes.punchline: Suggesting impossibly ambitious tasks for one hour
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina grandiosity - treating major achievements as casual tasks

jokes.text: I gotta run around like a Labrador or a guy on fire or something like that.
jokes.setup: Selina expressing her manic energy
jokes.punchline: Comparing herself to both a happy dog and a burning person
jokes.explanation: Brilliant manic Selina energy with absurd but vivid imagery

jokes.text: She's fucking nuts.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting that Selina seems excited
jokes.punchline: Mike's blunt assessment of her mental state
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Mike bluntness - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: They got the shakes so bad, I think I see the plane rattling.
jokes.setup: Mike describing the sober journalists
jokes.punchline: Their withdrawal symptoms being so severe they're shaking the plane
jokes.explanation: Great visual exaggeration about press corps alcoholism

jokes.text: These guys could be drinking jet fuel.
jokes.setup: Talking about desperate journalists needing alcohol
jokes.punchline: Suggesting they'd drink anything alcoholic, even jet fuel
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the alcoholism theme with dark comedy

jokes.text: I could, like, bang a nail into that wall with my gaze.
jokes.setup: Selina claiming to be super focused
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous metaphor of hammering nails with eye power
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Selina metaphor showing her manic state

jokes.text: That finishing sentences thing is... - Irritating? Very. But sometimes necessary when people are being slow or dull.
jokes.setup: Reporter complaining about Amy finishing sentences
jokes.punchline: Amy immediately doing it again while explaining why she does it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy moment - aggressive competence displayed through the very behavior being criticized

jokes.text: No people skills. Like a robot.
jokes.setup: Observing Amy's interpersonal style
jokes.punchline: The blunt robot comparison
jokes.explanation: Simple but accurate character observation - functional comedy

jokes.text: Maybe that's 'cause they invented numbers, huh?
jokes.setup: Mike explaining why Iranians want everything in triplicate
jokes.punchline: The historically inaccurate and absurd explanation about inventing numbers
jokes.explanation: Mike's casual ignorance presented as wisdom - good character moment

jokes.text: See that? Catlike reflexes.
jokes.setup: Mike presumably catching something
jokes.punchline: His proud assessment of his ordinary reflexes
jokes.explanation: Mike's delusional self-congratulation - solid character comedy

jokes.text: In my family, basketball is a religion. Also Catholicism. That's actually the main one.
jokes.setup: Jonah making a metaphor about basketball being religion
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly clarifying that they're actually Catholic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah awkwardness - starting confidently then backtracking

jokes.text: It's for my friend's son. - Who should he make it out to? - Sue Wilson. He's named after me.
jokes.setup: Getting an autograph for a friend's son
jokes.punchline: Revealing the 'son' is named Sue after Jonah
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing Jonah's real name is Sue - great character humor

jokes.text: L is for ladies who play tennis, G is for guys who do other guys, B is for bisexual... I couldn't think of one to go for that... and T... - Is for tucking it in or tacking it on.
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to remember LGBT terminology
jokes.punchline: His crude and inappropriate mnemonic device
jokes.explanation: Cringeworthy Jonah insensitivity - appropriate character moment but crude humor

jokes.text: Thanks for killing my joke and then mutilating it.
jokes.setup: Staff member correcting Jonah about transgender
jokes.punchline: Jonah's dramatically wounded response to the correction
jokes.explanation: Good Jonah self-pity and dramatic language - solid character work

jokes.text: Well, as my grandfather never said, let's go be inclusive.
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to quote his grandfather
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging his grandfather never said anything inclusive
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of inspirational quotes - good Jonah awkwardness

jokes.text: I could not be more likable if I had given both of my kidneys to some sick kid.
jokes.setup: Selina commenting on her polling numbers
jokes.punchline: The extreme hypothetical about kidney donation for likability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina grandiosity about her popularity - characteristically extreme metaphor

jokes.text: Ma'am, we're in a tea totalitarian state.
jokes.setup: Selina asking for champagne
jokes.punchline: Gary's mangled 'teetotaling' as 'tea totalitarian'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant malapropism from Gary - creates a new meaning while showing his confusion

jokes.text: I put the champagne in the water bottles. - You don't have to explain. I can tell what you did.
jokes.setup: Gary proudly explaining his smuggling technique
jokes.punchline: Selina's dry response that it's obvious
jokes.explanation: Good dynamic between Gary's pride and Selina's dismissiveness

jokes.text: The last surviving Golden Girl.
jokes.setup: Looking at Doyle after his dental work
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to an elderly sitcom character
jokes.explanation: Sharp visual insult with perfect pop culture reference - very quotable

jokes.text: A speech is a speech. Phonemes connecting to produce meanings.
jokes.setup: Asked if the speech needed reworking for the VP
jokes.punchline: Kent's robotic reduction of speeches to linguistic components
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent robotic thinking - reducing human communication to technical terms

jokes.text: When I was a... boy, it was my gay friends who taught me how to be tolerant and how to be true to yourself
jokes.setup: Doyle giving a speech about LGBTQ issues
jokes.punchline: His awkward pause and wrong word choice revealing discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Doyle's discomfort with the topic

jokes.text: and how to dance to Madonna. - Madonna? - What? Was I supposed to say that?
jokes.setup: Doyle continuing his awkward LGBTQ speech
jokes.punchline: His panicked realization that he said something wrong
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Doyle's discomfort - shows his cluelessness about cultural stereotypes

jokes.text: You know, back in college, my... girlfriends would... Boyfriends... my friends...
jokes.setup: Doyle trying to continue his personal anecdote
jokes.punchline: His verbal stumbling and confusion about what to call his friends
jokes.explanation: More Doyle cringe but getting repetitive - shows his complete inability to handle the topic

jokes.text: He is the asshole of an asshole's asshole.
jokes.setup: Mike describing Leon West
jokes.punchline: The escalating metaphor of nested assholes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating insult structure - very quotable and characteristically Mike

jokes.text: A face I never thought I'd want to kiss. - I still don't.
jokes.setup: Mike's greeting suggesting relief to see Leon
jokes.punchline: His immediate clarification that he's still not attracted
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline with Mike's characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: Yeah, I'll have something to say about it on TripAdvisor. - Worst minibar ever.
jokes.setup: Leon treating his detention like a hotel stay
jokes.punchline: Reducing his imprisonment to a customer service complaint
jokes.explanation: Good absurd understatement - treating imprisonment like bad hospitality

jokes.text: Both countries had to deny the visit, but now we confirm. Wait, both countries? Uh, his English isn't very good.
jokes.setup: Abbas explaining the diplomatic situation
jokes.punchline: Mike's obvious lie about Abbas's clearly good English
jokes.explanation: Good diplomatic awkwardness with Mike's terrible cover story

jokes.text: Not that you guys are planning one. I... ignore me. I don't know why I said that.
jokes.setup: Leon mentioning Air Force One can survive nuclear blasts
jokes.punchline: His panicked realization he implied Iran might nuke them
jokes.explanation: Good diplomatic awkwardness - Leon's mouth getting ahead of his brain

jokes.text: So let's celebrate all things L and GBT. Huh?
jokes.setup: Doyle trying to be inclusive in his speech
jokes.punchline: Forgetting the Q in LGBTQ and his confused reaction to correction
jokes.explanation: More Doyle cringe but becoming repetitive - shows his continued confusion

jokes.text: respect all people's rights to be a Q. Or rather just Q. Yeah, just Q.
jokes.setup: Doyle trying to correct his LGBTQ terminology
jokes.punchline: His increasingly confused attempts to get it right
jokes.explanation: Continues Doyle's confusion but getting repetitive - less impact than earlier moments

jokes.text: Sounds like he's learning a sex alphabet.
jokes.setup: Watching Doyle struggle with LGBTQ terminology
jokes.punchline: Amy's clinical assessment of his confused approach
jokes.explanation: Excellent Amy observation - clinical and cutting with perfect economy

jokes.text: I've seen a salmon in a grizzly's mouth look less panicky than that.
jokes.setup: Describing Doyle's panic during the speech
jokes.punchline: The vivid nature documentary metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Amy metaphor - vivid, original, and perfectly captures the panic level

jokes.text: Ironically, the straight guy is very stiff around the gay guy.
jokes.setup: Kent analyzing Doyle's performance
jokes.punchline: The double meaning of 'stiff' in context
jokes.explanation: Good Kent observation with sexual wordplay - clinical delivery makes it funnier

jokes.text: Yet again, the left hand has no idea what the right hand is doing and the freakish middle hand is punching me repeatedly in the tits.
jokes.setup: Amy complaining about lack of coordination
jokes.punchline: The absurd three-handed metaphor with violent imagery
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Amy rant - takes familiar metaphor and makes it brilliantly absurd and violent

jokes.text: But it's only been, like, six weeks and three days, so I'm not sweating it.
jokes.setup: Dan claiming he's not worried about finding work
jokes.punchline: The precise counting revealing he's obsessively tracking time
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dan's false confidence undermined by obsessive detail

jokes.text: I kind of feel like hitting on it.
jokes.setup: Dan commenting on how awesome the office is
jokes.punchline: Wanting to romantically pursue a building
jokes.explanation: Good Dan shallowness - treating impressive things as sexual objects

jokes.text: I can make you so rich, you can pay Bill Gates to give you a lap dance.
jokes.setup: Sidney promising to make Dan rich
jokes.punchline: The absurd specific scenario of paying the world's richest man for sexual services
jokes.explanation: Great absurd wealth metaphor - specific and memorable imagery

jokes.text: And fired. Yeah, don't forget fired. - Fired up...
jokes.setup: Sidney pointing out Dan's employment status
jokes.punchline: Dan's weak attempt to spin 'fired' into 'fired up'
jokes.explanation: Good Dan desperation showing through weak wordplay attempt

jokes.text: But are you ready to take a dump on Mike McLintock's doorstep?
jokes.setup: Sidney asking if Dan can work against his former colleagues
jokes.punchline: The crude literal metaphor for betrayal
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective metaphor for political betrayal

jokes.text: Like an initiation kind of thing? No, I mean, can you take on clients who are best served by you shitting on the Meyer agenda?
jokes.setup: Dan misunderstanding Sidney's metaphor as literal
jokes.punchline: Sidney's clarification that it's about political opposition, not actual defecation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Dan's literal-mindedness

jokes.text: Well, I'm sure food is the last thing on your mind. I know it's the last thing on my mind. - I wonder if you're hungry, though. - Yes, I am.
jokes.setup: Selina making assumptions about Leon's appetite
jokes.punchline: Her continuing to talk about food while claiming it's not on her mind
jokes.explanation: Good Selina contradiction - claiming not to think about something while obsessing over it

jokes.text: It's a... It's deodorant.
jokes.setup: Leon asking if something is food
jokes.punchline: Gary having to explain it's deodorant, implying Leon needs it
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment about Leon's hygiene - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Not fully into the blender. Maybe just a little bit. Just the toe, 'cause...
jokes.setup: Amy accusing Jonah of throwing Kent 'into the blender'
jokes.punchline: Jonah's attempt to minimize the betrayal with bizarre anatomical specificity
jokes.explanation: Good Jonah attempt to minimize his betrayal with absurd specificity

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I can ride her hard, hang her up wet.
jokes.setup: Amy asking how hard Jonah can work his media contact
jokes.punchline: Jonah's inappropriate sexual metaphor about professional relationship
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah inappropriateness - crude but character-appropriate

jokes.text: And I wish I could have visited every country in the whole world. You know? Even New Zealand.
jokes.setup: Selina expressing desire to visit every country
jokes.punchline: Treating New Zealand as an afterthought or lesser country
jokes.explanation: Good Selina casual dismissiveness - unintentionally insulting

jokes.text: So, just to clarify, you were delayed by a day. - Yeah. - My release was delayed by a day. Mm-hmm. Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Leon connecting the timing of events
jokes.punchline: Selina's nervous confirmation revealing the connection
jokes.explanation: Good building tension as Leon pieces together the truth

jokes.text: Christ, it's that data fuck.
jokes.setup: Learning about the Doyle story problem
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude assessment of Kent
jokes.explanation: Good Selina vulgarity - shows her stress and tendency to blame others

jokes.text: Give him a handjob if you have to, just get it done, okay?
jokes.setup: Selina telling someone to distract Leon
jokes.punchline: Suggesting sexual favors as a distraction technique
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's desperation and inappropriate problem-solving

jokes.text: That is bad. - Yeah. - That is bad. - It is bad. And Leon wanting to say it's bad is really bad. It's worse.
jokes.setup: Discussing how bad the Leon situation is
jokes.punchline: The escalating agreement about increasing levels of badness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but somewhat repetitive - shows panic building

jokes.text: Are you seriously detaining me again? Am I being rendered? No, you're being friendered.
jokes.setup: Leon asking if he's being detained again
jokes.punchline: Selina's made-up word 'friendered' as a euphemism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Selina neologism - perfectly captures her tendency to euphemize bad situations

jokes.text: So just please accept our compulsory hospitality.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to frame Leon's detention positively
jokes.punchline: The oxymoron of 'compulsory hospitality'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina diplomatic doublespeak - making imprisonment sound pleasant

jokes.text: My gosh, you look like a middle-school teacher.
jokes.setup: Sidney commenting on Dan's appearance
jokes.punchline: The specific, unflattering comparison to middle school teacher
jokes.explanation: Good specific insult - captures Dan's downward mobility

jokes.text: Finally, something good to watch on the idiot box.
jokes.setup: Dan being told he'll be on TV as a commentator
jokes.punchline: His arrogant assumption that he'll improve television
jokes.explanation: Good Dan arrogance - his inflated sense of his own value

jokes.text: You'll figure it out. Listen, you give me glacé cherries, and I'll give you a bed of money with pussy on the nightstand.
jokes.setup: Sidney telling Dan to work in glacé cherries somehow
jokes.punchline: The absurd reward of money bed with sexual services
jokes.explanation: Crude but vivid imagery of corrupting wealth - fits Sidney's character

jokes.text: an American she deliberately did not de-detain.
jokes.setup: Leon figuring out the detention scheme
jokes.punchline: His made-up term 'de-detain' to describe the deliberate delay
jokes.explanation: Good neologism showing Leon's journalistic precision with language

jokes.text: either get Leon's mom to the air base or buy yourself a very convincing wig.
jokes.setup: Amy giving Jonah instructions about Leon's arrival
jokes.punchline: The bizarre backup plan of Jonah in drag as Leon's mother
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd contingency plan - perfectly captures the desperation

jokes.text: It's 'Black Hawk Down' with Laurel and Hardy.
jokes.setup: Learning that Mike and Gary are stranded in Iran
jokes.punchline: Comparing the serious military situation to classic comedy duo
jokes.explanation: Exceptional comparison - perfectly captures the absurdity of the incompetent being in danger

jokes.text: Bill, of my various walking paces, I selected moderate to fast.
jokes.setup: Bill asking where Kent has been
jokes.punchline: Kent's robotic explanation of his walking speed selection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent robotic thinking - treating basic human activity as technical decision

jokes.text: Pardonne. Pardonne. - That's French!
jokes.setup: Mike trying to communicate in Iran
jokes.punchline: Gary pointing out he's speaking the wrong foreign language
jokes.explanation: Good panic-induced stupidity - shows how flustered they are

jokes.text: The man whose dream vacation is hunting the street kids of Rio on a human safari.
jokes.setup: Amy describing Sidney Purcell
jokes.punchline: The horrifically specific description of his recreational activities
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark character assassination - visceral and memorable

jokes.text: I've never had strong feelings for a plane, but right now I would cheat on Wendy with that thing.
jokes.setup: Mike expressing relief at finding the backup plane
jokes.punchline: His willingness to commit adultery with aircraft
jokes.explanation: Great Mike desperation expressed through absurd sexual metaphor

jokes.text: Uh, Teddy's been touching me. Or tea.
jokes.setup: Jonah being asked what's happening at VP's office
jokes.punchline: His nervous deflection to tea instead of sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Good Jonah nervousness but the setup is awkward

jokes.text: he cupped my testicles. On another occasion, he patted or tapped on my testicles. And then on another occasion, he held my testicles for a significantly long time.
jokes.setup: Jonah describing the harassment incidents
jokes.punchline: The increasingly detailed and uncomfortable escalation
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable escalation - gets laughs from awkwardness but feels exploitative

jokes.text: Guess what. I just got engaged. Are you fucking kidding me? Catherine's there, too?
jokes.setup: Catherine announcing engagement during crisis
jokes.punchline: Selina's horror that her daughter is also on the call
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of crisis with personal news at worst time

jokes.text: Okay, we got nothing out of that but a funny story.
jokes.setup: After the awkward call about sexual harassment
jokes.punchline: Selina's clinical assessment of getting entertainment value only
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the show's own dark humor approach

jokes.text: Jesus Christ, the Chinese can 3-D print a hundred houses in that time.
jokes.setup: Learning the plane repair will take a week
jokes.punchline: The exaggerated comparison to Chinese manufacturing speed
jokes.explanation: Good frustrated exaggeration with contemporary reference

jokes.text: In a dry country, the man with all the booze holds the cards.
jokes.setup: Gary explaining how they'll get on the press plane
jokes.punchline: His adaptation of the 'in the land of the blind' saying for alcoholics
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of classic saying for specific situation

jokes.text: Why in the name of pixelated fuck would you do that? My God!
jokes.setup: Selina learning about the data breach plan
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre censored profanity 'pixelated fuck'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Selina neologism - creates new profanity combining digital and analog censorship

jokes.text: use the word, I don't know, cupcake instead of data and use happy instead of bereaved or something.
jokes.setup: Selina asking for code words
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate cheerful words for discussing dead children
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - euphemizing horrific acts with pleasant words

jokes.text: in targeting happy parents after stealing cupcakes about their dead children.
jokes.setup: Using Selina's code words
jokes.punchline: The absurd result of euphemistic language applied to horrible acts
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how euphemisms make horrific acts sound nonsensical

jokes.text: I'm supposed to have my finger on the button. But for all I know, it's been rewired and I'm just operating some sort of light in a closet somewhere.
jokes.setup: Selina feeling out of control
jokes.punchline: The paranoid image of her nuclear authority being fake and trivial
jokes.explanation: Excellent Selina paranoia - reduces ultimate power to controlling a closet light

jokes.text: I'm gonna treat him like my own brother, ahem, who I had murdered back in '86.
jokes.setup: Selina saying she'll treat Leon like family
jokes.punchline: The casual admission of having her brother killed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark Selina humor - casual admission of fratricide delivered perfectly

jokes.text: You could say that the president's Iran visit puts a delicious glacé cherry on the cake of Selina Meyer's tour.
jokes.setup: Dan trying to work in the lobbying client reference
jokes.punchline: The awkward forced insertion of glacé cherries into political commentary
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Dan's desperation to please his new boss

jokes.text: That is Cirque du Soleil suicide bombing.
jokes.setup: Amy describing the elaborate self-sabotage
jokes.punchline: The comparison to elaborate acrobatic self-destruction
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Amy metaphor - combines high art with self-destruction perfectly

jokes.text: I feel like I'm on a life-support machine and they keep pulling the plug to charge their phones.
jokes.setup: Amy expressing her frustration with the situation
jokes.punchline: The modern metaphor of having life support interrupted for phone charging
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modern metaphor - combines life-and-death stakes with mundane selfishness

jokes.text: We've got about 10 seconds to drink everything and then eat the bottles.
jokes.setup: Trying to hide alcohol from Iranian authorities
jokes.punchline: The escalation to consuming glass containers
jokes.explanation: Good panic escalation - absurd solution to immediate problem

jokes.text: More minds the merrier. That's a saying, I think.
jokes.setup: Ben responding to news of bringing in another advisor
jokes.punchline: His uncertain attempt at wisdom while clearly dying inside
jokes.explanation: Good Ben defeat - trying to be positive while clearly devastated

jokes.text: My specialty... Common sense.
jokes.setup: Karen introducing herself as the new senior advisor
jokes.punchline: The implied criticism that the administration lacks basic common sense
jokes.explanation: Good subtle dig at existing team's competence

jokes.text: Hey, Catherine, about earlier. - The molesting. - What?
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to clarify something from earlier
jokes.punchline: Catherine's shocking misunderstanding of what he's referring to
jokes.explanation: Good shocking misdirection but relies on uncomfortable subject matter

jokes.text: I'm not gonna make it to the cottage, Wendy. I'm in Iran.
jokes.setup: Mike calling his girlfriend about their weekend plans
jokes.punchline: The casual delivery of being stranded in a foreign country
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike understatement - treating international crisis as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: No, you're not. - Yes, we are. - But we are. I'm 48. Put your hand down.
jokes.setup: Catherine announcing engagement
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissal and Catherine's defensive age declaration
jokes.explanation: Good mother-daughter dynamic with Selina's controlling nature

jokes.text: I'm not snacking. I'm on my diet. It's a celery stick. All right, it's a cookie.
jokes.setup: Selina defending her eating
jokes.punchline: The quick progression from health food to junk food admission
jokes.explanation: Good Selina self-deception and quick backtracking
